titlestmt.titleproper: Arecibo Ionospheric Observatory and Electrical Engineering reports16-6-3333
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Maria A. Keating

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-04-16 18:09:55 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Arecibo Ionospheric Observatory and Electrical Engineering reports
did.abstract: Cornell University School of Electrical Engineering technical, scientific and quarterly progress reports, 1950-1960. Arecibo Ionospheric Observatory pamphlet, 1962. Center for Radiophysics and Space Research research reports, 1960-1969; quarterly status reports to the Office of Naval Research, 1963-1969; proposals to Office of Naval Research, 1961-1964; progress report to the National Science Foundation, 1956-1957; and annual report, 1974-1975. The Antenna Laboratory reports from the Dept. of Electrical Engineering, Ohio State University, 1951-1953; report on "High Resolution Studies of Discrete Radio Sources," University of California, San Diego, 1968.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Cornell University School of Electrical Engineering technical, scientific and quarterly progress reports, 1950-1960. Arecibo Ionospheric Observatory pamphlet, 1962. Center for Radiophysics and Space Research research reports, 1960-1969; quarterly status reports to the Office of Naval Research, 1963-1969; proposals to Office of Naval Research, 1961-1964; progress report to the National Science Foundation, 1956-1957; and annual report, 1974-1975. The Antenna Laboratory reports from the Dept. of Electrical Engineering, Ohio State University, 1951-1953; report on "High Resolution Studies of Discrete Radio Sources," University of California, San Diego, 1968.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Arecibo Ionospheric Observatory and Electrical Engineering report, #16-6-3333. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Radio astronomy -- United States -- Research.
controlaccess.corpname: Cornell University. School of Electrical Engineering
Ohio State University. School of Electrical Engineering

did.unittitle: Cornell University's Center for Radiophysics and Space Research Publications
"Thermal Radio Emission in an Ionized Gas"
"Scientific Experiments for the Arecibo Radio Observatory"
"Range, Declination, and Doppler-Shift Calculations for an Interplanetary Radar"
"Reflection of Radio Waves from the Sun in the Presence of the Magnetic Field of a Sunspot"
"Measurement of Faraday Dispersion in Type III Solar Events"
"Proposal to Office of Naval Research for Extension of Contract Nonr 401 (27) from December 1, 1961 to June 15, 1962"
"Construction of the Department of Defense Ionospheric Research Facility - III"

"Proposed Experiments for the Arecibo Ionospheric Observatory"
"Construction of the Department of Defense Ionospheric Research Facility - VI"

"Quarterly Status Report to the Office of Naval Research on the Measurement of Solar Radio Radiation at 430 Megacycles, ONR Contract Nonr 401 (27), January 1, 1964 through March 31, l964"
"Proposal to the Office of Naval Research for the Measurement of Solar Radio Radiation at 430 Megacycles, Continuation of ONR Contract Nonr-401(27), August 1, 1964 through July 31, 1965"

"Quarterly Status Report to the Office of Naval Research on the Measurement of Solar Radio Radiation at 430 Megacycles, ONR Contract Nonr 401(27), August 1, 1964 through October 31, l964"
"Quarterly Status Report to the Office of Naval Research on the Measurement of Solar Radiation at 430 Megacycles, ONR Contract Nonr 401 (27), November 1, 1964 through January 31, l965"
"Radiometric Observations of Jupiter, Venus, and Mars at 430 Mc/s"
"Quarterly Status Report to the Office of Naval Research on the Measurement of Solar Radiation at 430 Megacycles, ONR Contract Nonr 401 (27), August 1, 1965 through April 30, l966"
"Final Report to the Office of Naval Research on the Measurement of Solar Radiation at 430 Megacycles, ONR Contract Nonr 401(27)"
"High Resolution Studies of Discrete Radio Sources" (University of California, San Diego Publication)
"A High-Performance Line Source Feed for the AIO Spherical Reflector"
"Annual Report, Center for Radiophysics and Space Research, July 1, 1974 - June 30, 1975"
Cornell University's School of Electrical Engineering Publications
"A Solution for the Path of Radio Waves through the Solar Atmosphere by Elliptic Integrals"
"The Faraday Effect in the Earth's Ionosphere with Special Reference to Polarization Measurements of Solar Radio Emission"
"First Progress Report on National Science Foundation Grant No. G 2549, Polarization Dispersion of the 200 mc Bursts from the Sun for the Year 1 July 1956 to 30 June 1957"
"Radio Noise and Atmospheric Refraction at VHF and UHF"
"Limitations on the Accuracy of Angular Location of a Radio Star Using Interferometer Techniques"
"Radio Noise and Atmospheric Refraction at VHF and UHF"
"Antenna Beam Swinging and the Spherical Reflector"
"Reflection of Radio Waves from the Sun"
"Correlator for Noise Signals with Application to 200 mc/s Polarimeter"
Ohio State University's Antenna Laboratory Publications
"Aperture Admittances and Propagation Constants of Metal-Loaded Waveguides"
"The Aperture Admittance of a Rectangular Waveguide Radiating Into Half-Space"
"An Investigation of Pulsed Radar Systems for Model Measurements"
